title: Retention Delete Verification
description: Plan and run focused checks for expiry and deletion propagation across primary and derived stores.
body: Inputs: storage schema, cleanup job, account deletion path, indexes, caches, and analytics sinks. Use when personal data storage or deletion behavior changes. Do not promise deletion from unmanaged processors or backups without evidence. Procedure: identify copies, run local delete or expiry path, inspect derived stores, and record asynchronous or manual gaps. Output: verification evidence and blockers. Human gate: regulated data remains in an unhandled copy. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: run the documented inspection and validation workflow; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
